methods.method_name: Adding Age to Linear Mixed-Effects Model
methods.description: A statistical approach to correct for nonuniform age distribution bias by incorporating age as an explanatory variable in a linear mixed-effects model to analyze growth changes over time.

steps.description: Add age as a second explanatory variable to the linear mixed-effects model.
steps.searchable_summary: add age variable to linear mixed-effects model
outputs: adjusted model with age effect removed

steps.description: Analyze the outcome of the model to determine growth changes over time.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze model outcome for growth changes

methods.method_name: Excluding Species with Nonuniform Age Distributions
methods.description: A method to evaluate growth trends by excluding species with nonuniform age distributions to avoid biases in the analysis.

steps.description: Identify and exclude species with strongly nonuniform age distributions from the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude species with nonuniform age distributions

steps.description: Analyze growth trends for the remaining species after exclusion.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze growth trends for filtered species
